q: A macromolecule is a very large molecule that contains thousands of atoms or more.
Carbohydrates and nucleic acids are synthetic polymers manufactured in industry.
A homopolymer is a polymer that consists of only one kind of monomers.
A copolymer is a polymer formed from only one kind of monomers.
Addition polymerisation occurs when unsaturated monomers join together without losing any molecules or atoms.
Condensation polymerisation occurs with the addition of a small molecule such as water.
The simplest addition polymer is polyethene or polyethylene (PE).
The number of ethene molecules in each polyethene molecule varies from 100 to 300.
Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is produced by the addition polymerisation of chloroethene.
In polyalkenes made of one type of monomer, the repeat unit is different from the monomer unit.
Addition polymers lose plasticity and elasticity and become brittle at low temperatures.
Polythene is a natural polymer found in silk.
Terylene is an example of condensation polymers formed from terephthalic acid and ethylene glycol.
Terylene is a polyamide because it contains many amide linkages.
Nylon 6,6 is formed from the reaction of adipic acid and 1,6-diaminohexane.
Nylon is a polyester because it contains many ester linkages.
Proteins are made from thousands of amino acid monomers.
There are 50 different amino acids commonly found in proteins.
The covalent bond formed between two amino acids is called a peptide bond (amide link).
Condensation polymers often have higher molecular masses than addition polymers.
Plastics are non-biodegradable and can take hundreds of years to decompose.
Burning PVC produces non-toxic gases like oxygen.
Sea animals such as turtles often mistake plastic bags for food and choke on them.
Soil pollution by plastics only lasts for a few months.
The easiest way to deal with plastics is to reuse or recycle them.
Recycling symbols on plastics consist of three arrows around one of ten numbers.
Biodegradable plastics contain small amounts of starch granules to help decompose naturally.
Polylactic acid (PLA) is a petroleum-based plastic.
Photodegradable plastics incorporate carbonyl groups ($C=O$) along their chain to absorb energy from sunlight.
Starch is a synthetic polymer used in the production of PVC pipes.
Frying pans are coated with a layer of teflon because it is heat-resistant and has non-stick properties.
Polystyrene (styrofoam) is a heavy and durable plastic used for flooring mats.
PET stands for polyethylene terephthalate.
V/PVC stands for polyvinyl acetate.
LDPE stands for low-density polyethylene.
PS stands for polypropylene.
Adipic acid and 1,6-diaminohexane are the monomers of nylon 6,6.
The repeat unit of polyethene is $-(CH_{2}-CHCl)-$.
The presence of polar functional groups on condensation polymer chains often enhances chain-chain attractions.
Terylene is very weak and is not used in textiles.
